mnemonics.title: DESIGN PRODUCTION Steps
mnemonics.content: Remember the workflow: D-S-P-O-D-E (Define specs, Set up system, Prepare assets, Organize files, Document, Export/Deploy)

mnemonics.title: Design System Components
mnemonics.content: P-T-C-V: Principles, Tokens, Components, Variants - all elements of a comprehensive design system.

mnemonics.title: Handoff Checklist
mnemonics.content: F-S-A-C: Files, Specs, Assets, Code snippets - what designers must provide for handoff.

acronyms.usage: Used to style web pages and can be generated from design tools during handoff.
Used to connect design components with backend functionality.
Vector image format ideal for icons and UI graphics in production.
Color model used for digital screen display of design elements.
Color code format commonly used in web design specifications.

acronyms.expansion: Finite Element Analysis, Graphical Interactive Mac Applications (Backronym)
acronyms.usage: Popular design tool for creating, prototyping, and handoff of UI designs.
File format for layered design files in Adobe Photoshop.
The visual elements users interact with in a digital product.
The overall experience of a user interacting with a product.
Measure of image resolution important for print and high-density displays.

visualTips.visualization: Think of tokens as a pyramid: Base tokens (raw values like #FFFFFF) → Semantic tokens (meaningful like 'primary-color') → Component tokens (context-specific like 'button-bg').
Visualize breakpoints as a staircase: Mobile (bottom) → Tablet (middle) → Desktop (top) - design adapts as you climb.
Think of it as a relay race: Designer holds the baton (design) → passes to developer (handoff) → developer runs to finish line (implementation).
Imagine buttons as people with different moods: Default (neutral), Hover (excited), Active (pressed), Disabled (sleeping).

analogies.analogy: Like a recipe book - ingredients (tokens) + cooking instructions (components) = consistent meals (UI) every time.
Like assembling IKEA furniture - every screw and board must be exactly where the manual specifies for the final product to work.
Like Google Docs revision history - you can see who changed what, when, and restore previous versions if needed.
Like a brand's fashion rulebook - what colors to wear, what fonts to use, how to present consistently.
Like an architect handing over blueprints to construction workers - detailed plans ensure the building matches the vision.
Like water taking different container shapes - design flows and adapts to fill whatever screen it's displayed on.
